title: Inc/dec segmentation fault and compile error in Ariane project
body:
Dear Verilator Team, I am working on an Ariane project and using a verilator tool. However, for now, I am struggling with `make verilate`. Following is the description of the problem.

I have installed riscv-gnu-toolchain, riscv-tool with the latest versions available, updated Ariane submodules recursively, installed Verilator with the latest (4.035) version globally from the git master branch, unset $VERILATOR_ROOT by every built, have passed Verilator test examples successfully, downloaded some packages that advised by Verilator page that can help to improve performance of the builds, and so far it seems that I have done everything right following manuals of both [Ariane](https://github.com/pulp-platform/ariane) and [Verilator](https://github.com/verilator/verilator). Also, I am not using SystemC, so I did not download that package, if it may be the case. However, for my Ubuntu 18.04 OS, I can't `make verilate` with `Segmentation fault (core dumped) %Error: Command Failed` error. There are plenty of warnings on pin assignments, delays and expected values which you may see in the attached terminal log file.

Could you help me with the solution of this problem? Initially, I thought that it could be because of my RAM size (6GB) being small, however, it seems it is not the case.
I have googled around old issues, but for now, I did not find anything useful.
Hope to hear back from you soon.

[make_verilate_log.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4708136/make_verilate_log.txt)
